L1.area: HTTP request routing and reverse proxy handling

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where the source IP address of incoming HTTP requests can be spoofed by injecting crafted headers. This allows unauthenticated remote attackers to impersonate trusted local clients and execute arbitrary commands as root.

L3.area: Gateway reverse proxy routing and client IP resolution
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where the source IP address of incoming HTTP requests forwarded through the gateway reverse proxy can be spoofed by injecting crafted forwarding headers. The gateway does not validate or sanitize client IP information before proxying requests to backend services, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to impersonate trusted local clients and execute arbitrary commands as root.

ground_truth.reason: The gateway reverse proxy does not validate or rewrite the X-Forwarded-For header before proxying requests, allowing remote attackers to spoof their source IP as a trusted local address and bypass authentication checks that exempt localhost clients.
